body: ${2:Extended description explaining the logic, validation steps, or}
:return: A dictionary containing the processing results:
* **success** (bool): ``True`` if the operation completed without errors.
* **errorcode** (str): A unique slug identifying the failure reason.
* **${9:data_id}** (int): The internal database ID of the affected record.
* **details** (dict): Verbose error context or internal API responses.
:raises ${10:ExceptionType}: ${11:Describe conditions that trigger a hard exception.}
The passed arguments were incomplete or failed type validation.
A required linked record or configuration was not found.
${16:Detail any state changes, such as quantity updates or log entries.}
api_docstring_template.description: Profound Sphinx-style API docstring with formal error handling and security blocks.

appsession_get.description: Read the specified key from the users appsession

description: Write the specified key with the given content
to the users appsession
Create a log message. You can greb for your marker (first argument) in journalctl.
Pass your variables to the function for debugging.

body: printer_id=${3:printer_id  # Removing this will only show the label on screen},
type=${4:'raw'  # Remove this when outputting to screen},
description: Print a label for a data record.
The label will be printed for the record that matches the passed unique columns.
Usually you should to pass the ID.
If argument printer_id is not given the label will only be shown on screen.
Set argument type to raw to print actual labels.
Remove type argument to show labels on screen.
Deletes a data record in the given table with the given id.
ID argument can be replaced with another unique column of the table.
You can pass all arguments to db_dyn_delte that you cann pass to db_dyn_read.
Returns dictionary with:
['success'] Boolean on successful deletion
['data'] Will contain the deleted id
['msg'] Will contain any error message
Insert or update a data record for the given table.
Unique columns or an ID must be passed for updating the correct record.
Pass your columns for editing with the value that should be set.
Pass your columns without the tablename (<table>_<colname>).
Returns dictionary with:
['success'] Boolean on successful editing,
['data'] None when successful although documentation about that seems flaky,
['msg'] Will contain any error message
Loads a data record for the given table and the given id.
ID argument can be replaced with another unique column of the table.
Unique columns must be passed as a parameter in this case.
Returns dictionary with:
['success'] Boolean when anything was found,
['data'] Contains the found row.
Each row contains a dictionary with all columns (fullcolumn name).
['msg'] Will contain any error message
Loads all data records that match the given search criteria.
You can also provide an order by statement in pure sql syntax.
Pass your columns without the tablename (<table>_<colname>).
Returns dictionary with:
['success'] Boolean when anything was found,
['data'] List of found rows.
Each row contains a dictionary with all columns (fullcolumn name).
['msg'] Will contain any error message
Insert or update a data set for the given table.
Identifying values must be passed via the 'ident' mapping to uniquely match a row.
Values to be inserted or updated are passed in 'payload'.
Only columns with full names (e.g. <table>_<column>) are accepted.
Returns dictionary with:
['success']: Boolean indicating whether the operation succeeded,
['errormessage']: String with error details if success is False,
['src']: The generated SQL query if 'src__' is set,
['<TBL>_id']: ID of the inserted or updated row
